typography.typesetting: Use tight letter spacing (`tracking-tight` or `tracking-tighter`) for headings > 32px. Use uppercase, tiny size, and wide spacing (`tracking-[0.2em]`) for overlines, labels, and small CTAs. Do not use Inter or generic default fonts. Maintain high legibility for product prices and numbers.

colors.strategy: Archetype 1: Organic & Earthy meets Minimalist Swiss. Black/White/Neutral foundation where the product is the hero, with brand colors (like Cyber Days promos) configurable via admin.
colors.base: hsl(0, 0%, 100%) [Pure White] and hsl(0, 0%, 98%) [Stone/Off-White] for section distinction.
colors.text: hsl(220, 20%, 10%) [Dark Charcoal] for primary text. Avoid pure black.
colors.primary: hsl(220, 20%, 10%) by default, but meant to be dynamically injected via Admin CMS.
colors.admin_config: The application must support CSS variables for the primary brand color so the Admin can customize it. Example: `--primary: 220 20% 10%`.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Use 'Glassmorphism' (Crystal variant) for sticky headers: `bg-white/70 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-black/5`. For product cards, use flat backgrounds with no borders, or a very subtle 1px border (`border-black/5`) without shadows.
visual_enhancers.shadows: E-commerce front-end: Minimal to no shadows. Rely on spacing and typography for hierarchy. Admin Panel: Use 'Grid Borders' or subtle 1px borders with very soft ambient shadows (`shadow-sm`) on hover.
visual_enhancers.textures: Consider a very subtle noise overlay for the hero section to kill digital flatness, but keep product images perfectly clean.

layouts.spacing: Generous spacing. Use `py-24` or `py-32` for sections. Container padding should be `px-6 md:px-12`. Negative space is critical to make it feel premium.
layouts.grids: E-commerce Home: Use an asymmetric 'Bento' grid for Cyber Days banners (e.g., 1 large feature, 2 smaller supporting promos). Product Catalog: Standard 2-col (mobile) to 4-col (desktop) uniform grids.
layouts.admin_grids: High Density Grid (Mode B). Dense, functional, strict grid hierarchy using `gap-4` or `gap-6`.

motion.animations: Use `framer-motion` for page transitions. Product images must have a subtle hover scale (`group-hover:scale-105 transition-transform duration-500 ease-out`).
motion.interactions: Micro-interactions on all buttons and interactive elements. Staggered fade-up entrances for product lists.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text on hero images is readable (use subtle gradients or text shadows). Do not use transparent backgrounds without blur/scrim for dropdowns or navs.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="yape-checkout-button"`, `data-testid="admin-save-brand-color"`).

components.buttons: Sharp, elegant buttons. No excessive border-radius. Use `rounded-sm` or `rounded-none`. CTAs should stand out using the primary brand color.
components.inputs: Clean, minimalist inputs. Consider a variant with only a bottom border for a high-end fashion feel, or standard Shadcn inputs customized with `rounded-sm`.
components.checkout: Frictionless 2-column layout. When 'Yape/Plin' is selected, display a crisp, clean modal or inline section with the QR code and clear instructions to upload the voucher. Include an elegant 'Upload Receipt' dropzone.
components.admin_panel: Use Shadcn UI customized to be dense. Cardless or 4px radius. High contrast for readability.

image_urls.description: Elegant display of handmade ceramic clay art pieces, for minimalist hero banner background.
Portrait of a fashionable African American woman, alternative hero/promo banner.
Stylish flat lay of a leather handbag with luxury accessories.
White vase sitting on top of a table.
2 white ceramic mugs on white table.
Top view of a woman crafting white ceramic pottery pieces. Use for 'About Us' or category banner.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a React frontend (use .jsx/.js, NOT .tsx/.ts) and FastAPI backend with MongoDB.
Do NOT use 'Inter' for headings. Install and use 'Outfit' (headings) and 'DM Sans' (body) via Google Fonts.
Implement Yape/Plin payment flow clearly: Display QR code -> Provide file upload for voucher -> Show 'Pending Validation' state.
Create an Admin Panel route that manages Products (CRUD), reviews Orders/Vouchers, and configures the store's primary theme color (saved to backend and applied dynamically via CSS variables).
Install 'framer-motion' for page transitions and micro-interactions.
Ensure the layout is highly responsive, optimizing for mobile users in Peru on 4G/5G connections.
Apply 'data-testid' to ALL buttons, inputs, and functional UI elements.
Use Shadcn components, but heavily customize them to match the minimalist, elegant e-commerce vibe (e.g., override rounded-md to rounded-sm, use flat designs).
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Avoid generic 'AI slop' aesthetics. Do not use standard centered, boring layouts. Embrace asymmetry and generous negative space.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy, but keep styling flat and elegant for this project.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) for sticky navs and overlays.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Add transitions for specific interactive elements only.
You MUST NOT center align the app container. Do not add '.App { text-align: center; }'. Keep natural reading flow.
Always use modern button styles (sharp rounded-sm) with interaction animations and explicit hover color states.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...). Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...}).
For the Yape/Plin flow, ensure the QR code is clearly visible and the file uploader for the receipt is extremely user-friendly.
